title: haplopappus racemosus var. brachycephalus
intro: Haplopappus racemosus var. brachycephalus, also known as the prairie arnica or mountain sunflower, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to North America and has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and potentially even inhibiting the growth of certain cancer cells. Haplopappus racemosus var. brachycephalus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and its flowers and leaves can be used fresh or dried for topical applications.
